{{CargoRelic
|name=Garns Number Place
|class_id=22403
|rank=Blue
|affct=FAME
|birthday=02-12
|source=Lottery Machine No.3 (0.25%)
|description=On Feb 12, 1880 AD, retired architect Garns invented this number-filling puzzle before it was named Sudoku and became popular worldwide.<br>As the predecessor of Sudoku, the number-filling puzzle of Latin squares was invented by Euler and other mathematicians in the early 18th century. After nearly a century of evolution, the game was eventually brought to Japan by a scholar and renamed Sudoku, becoming a classic puzzle game.
|fame=48
|art=27
|fth=23
|civ=26
|tech=30
|ss1=Per 10,000 Fervor, Priest-Snail Assistance DMG+8<br>Fervor: 500000/500000
|ss2=Per 10,000 Fervor, Priest-Snail Assistance DMG+16<br>Fervor: 500000/500000
|ss3=Per 10,000 Fervor, Priest-Snail Assistance DMG+24<br>Fervor: 500000/500000
|ss4=Per 10,000 Fervor, Priest-Snail Assistance DMG+40<br>Fervor: 500000/500000
|ss7=Per 10,000 Fervor, Priest-Snail Assistance DMG+40<br>Fervor: 500000/500000<br>Protomon Snail assist DMG + 400 (Awakened)
|skill1=[[File:Genesim skill I.png|50px]]Place in Gene Sim Device: This Relic's FAME +5
}}
